w:r.w:t: Mid-Range Carbon Raiser — Low Sulphur · Low Nitrogen · Cost-Effective Alternative to
Graphite Electrode Scrap — Mid-Range Carbon Raiser | Low Sulphur · Low Nitrogen | Cost-Effective Alternative to GPC
Sub-headline: Fixed Carbon ≥98–99.5% · S ≤0.08% · N ≤0.05% · Absorption 88–94% · Sizes 1–25mm · Imported from China
Graphite electrode scrap is the graphitic waste — offcuts, trimmings and machining debris — gener
ated during the manufacture and processing of graphite electrodes for EAF furnaces. After crushing, cleaning and screening to
sizes, it yields a material that has passed through full
as GPC — but at a meaningfully lower cost because it is a by-product rather than a primary manufactured product.
The result: a carbon raiser with chemistry close to GPC — very high F.C., low S,
at a price 20–35% below standard GPC. This makes ele
ctrode scrap the practical mid-range choice for cost-conscious buyers who need better quality than anthracite but cannot justify full GPC pricing on every heat.
Quality EAF steelmaking, standard ductile iron
Ladle trim additions, small IF additions
Quality consistency is the most critical issue — it varies by electrode grade (RP, HP, UHP) and manufacturing origin; HP/UHP origin gives lower and more consistent S/N values
S and N are significantly better than anthracite — electrode scrap is suitable fo
r standard ductile iron where GPC's ultra-low N is not mandatory
Cost vs GPC: 20–35% cheaper per MT at similar F.C. and S levels — strong value for cost-sensitive buyers
COA per lot: F.C., S, N, Ash, VM, Moisture, size analysis — we pre-qualify suppliers b
Section 4 — Electrode scrap vs GPC — when to choose which
You produce general-purpose carbon or low-alloy steel where S ≤0.08% and N ≤0.05% meets your specification
You produce grey iron or standard ductile i
ron where N 0.03–0.05% is within your Mg treatment window
You want significantly better quality than anthracite at a lower cost than GPC
You have a reliable source with consistent HP/UHP electrode origin
You produce premium ductile iron (ADI, thin-wall SG iron) where N ≤0.01% is essential
You produce bearing steel, spring steel, stainless or tool steel — every ppm of N matters
Long-term quality defect costs outweigh the per-MT price saving
EAF steelmaking: primary use — added during charging or as trim; standard and premium grades active across Raipur,
and other EAF clusters; buyers who experienced quality issues with anthracite often switch to electrode scrap as the
Induction furnace steelmaking: strong demand from mid-size IF units (20–50T furnaces) in
, Rajkot; buyers needing better quality than GCA without paying full GPC pricing for commodity billet production
y iron foundries: cupola and induction furnace grey iron; consistent carbon equivalent control without the nitrogen penalty of anthracite
Ductile iron foundries (standard grade): suitable where S ≤0.08% and N ≤0.05% is acceptable in your Mg treatment pract
ice; for premium ductile iron applications, use premium electrode scrap or GPC
Ladle additions (fine powder): 0–1mm grade added to ladle during tapping for precise carbon trim — fast dissolution, controllable addition quantity
Source selection by electrode grade — HP/UHP origin preferred for tighter S/N
Full COA per lot: F.C., S, N, Ash, VM, Moisture, size — third-party verified
Both standard and premium grades — match quality to your application
SGS / Intertek pre-shipment inspection available on request
Can be consolidated in same FCL with GPC — saves per-MT freight
MOQ 2 MT — easy trial before committing to full container
20–35% cheaper than GPC at comparable F.C. and S levels
Ex-stock standard 1–5mm at Indian port — delivery 3–7 days
Competitive CIF / DAP pricing to your plant
Payment on committed timelines — no delays
Section 7 — Graphite Electrode Scrap Exporters — Le
Good electrode scrap is harder to source consistently than most buyers realise — if you have reliable HP or UHP origin material with verifiable COAs, India is a ready market and we are a ready buyer.
India's steel and foundry sector runs on

— and electrode scrap sits in the sweet spot between anthracite (cheap but dirty) and GPC (clean but expensive). Buyers who have outgrown anthracite but are watching margins come to electrode scrap. Th
at means steady, growing demand — and we
We offer D/P or open account terms to suppliers who qualify with consistent COAs. One thing we've learned: suppliers who require LC
on every consignment lose out to those
who don't — because Indian importers have options, and workable payment terms is often the deciding factor. If your terms are flexible,
find us a reliable, growing buyer.
F.C. ≥98.0% (SGS/BV COA — not just manufacturer's own)
Electrode origin specified: RP / HP / UHP — HP/UHP strongly preferred for lower S/N variability
Sizes: 1–5mm primary · 3–8mm · 5–25mm secondary · clean, no clay or soil contamination
Packing: 25kg bags in 1MT jumbo bags o
Send us: Company · Location · Monthly capacity (MT) · Electrode origin (RP/HP/UHP) · F.C
S/N % · FOB price · Payment terms offered · Contact
electrode scrap India · electrode scrap carbon raiser India · low sulphur carbon a
India · electrode scrap EAF India · carbon raiser foundry India · graphite carbon additive supplier India · low nitrogen
Q1. What is graphite electrode scrap and whe
It is graphitic waste — offcuts and machining debris from graphite electrode manufacturing — crushed and screened to
sizes. It provides near-GPC quality carbon at 20–35% lower cost because it is a by-product, not a primar
Q2. Is electrode scrap quality consistent — or does it vary?
Quality varies more than GPC because it depends on electrode grade (RP, HP,
) and manufacturing source — HP/UHP origin gives lower and more consistent S and N values.
We pre-qualify all suppliers by electrode grade and provide
) suits general ductile iron where your Mg treatment window allows it; for premium du
ctile iron (ADI, thin-wall) where N must be ≤0.01%, switch to GPC. Share your casting spec and we confirm which grade applies.
Q4. What is the cost saving vs GPC and is the quality trade-off worth it?
Electrode scrap is 20–35% cheaper per MT than GPC at c
omparable F.C. and S levels — for general EAF steelmaking and standard ductile iron the saving is real and the quality is sufficient. For premium N-sensitive applications, use GPC.
Q5. What is the MOQ and how quickly can I get a sample?
es with full COA (F.C., S, N, Ash, VM, Moisture, size) are dispatched within 3–7 days. We can also consolidate electrode scrap with GPC in the same container to reduce per-MT freight cost.
